generatedSentence: Professor Lena Petrova, a woman whose face bore the lines of a life taught in the archives, felt a familiar uncertainty examining the latest acquisition at the private museum. A grotesque monkey statue, supposedly a relic from a mothership cult, had recently neighbored the exhibit dedicated to intergalactic infrastructure. Its optical signature, captured by Lena’s hidden camera, was a key ingredient in the unfolding mystery. She saw the mockery in the director's public joking, knowing his true nature was that of a ruthless judge presiding over a tyranny of secrets. The collection, a borrowed assemblage of various alien artifacts, seemed to abandon all pretense of legitimate research. An aching suspicion grew in Lena's gut; something sinister was between the lines of the corporate biography that masked the organization's true goals, an element sparking a dangerous uptick in the galactic power struggle, with Lena herself reluctantly engaged. Operating in the neutral zone of the cosmic neighbourhood, Lena discovered a covert facility where a complex calculation of resource exploitation was underway. She found evidence of counterfeit energy cells, protected by a shimmering futuristic ceinture field. A sharp anomaly pricked her sensor when she scanned a discarded wrapper, hinting at an imminent system rupture. The facility, run by powerful earners operating with unequivocal intent for termination of competitors, utilized advanced tech, even a high-security veterinary lab for biological experiments. Lena, her holster heavy with her plasma pistol, valued honesty above all. She navigated through rooms filled with false backgrounds and encrypted baggage, past silent watchdog drones. The sterile environment, reminiscent of old military  barracks, was broken only by a holographic sunflower display. A chilling interrogation of a captured operative, whom she had expertly whacked into submission, revealed their grand scheme to produce a weaponized virus, exploiting humanity’s vulnerability through an electric grid of hidden emitters. Lena knew she had only a jiffy until their plan reached the outer colonies. She spotted the lead scientist, a masculine figure in a crisp uniform, directing the final calibration of nanotechnology-infused rockets. He was the patron of this ignoble project, a former academic who had tricked many into his twisted vision of homeschooling his elite operatives. Lena, with a decisive jerking motion, socked a nearby guard, grabbed a towel from a utility cart, and began to hotwire the system. The scientist, as rigid as tofu, watched as Lena seized control, aiming to provoke a system overload. She exploited the network’s hidden properties, diverting massive power to create a controlled waste surge. The facility's control department was in chaos. She could not afford to be timid now; this was a turnkey operation she had to keep intact to avoid a greater disaster, reshaping her perspective on the fight. The tantrum of the failing system sent echoes through the facility, a diabolical symphony of defeat. Lena, feeling utterly knackered, knew she had achieved a precarious victory, saving the kindred spirits of the outer worlds from a fate worse than any horrors. The schoolmaster-turned-villain, now subdued, revealed the entire plot was a gambler's desperate play, a last sacrifice of their resources. He had planned to launch a single torpedo, codenamed "The Nymphet," from the main cockpit, believing it held the knack to turn the tide. Lena, seeing the negatives of this entire operation, understood that justice was a constant struggle. She flicked a final switch, cutting all power to the remaining systems, ensuring the virgin territory remained untouched. The quiet hum of the now-safe facility was a stark contrast to the chaotic jellyfishes of information that once filled the screens. She thought of a peaceful weekend, a simple grocery run, and how even small actions could prevent a catastrophic fault, proving that icons of heroism were not always grand, but often quiet.

imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, neo-noir comic art style, thick white gutters, clean black borders. The protagonist is a grizzled, middle-aged woman with a stern expression, deep-set eyes, and streaks of gray in her dark, pulled-back hair, wearing a worn but functional tech-suit. TOP-LEFT PANEL: Lena Petrova, a clearly adult woman with facial wrinkles, her face etched with concentration, examining strange optical readings on a handheld scanner in a dimly lit, futuristic museum exhibit, full body shot. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: Lena Petrova, her expression grim, discovering a hidden, high-tech lab beneath the museum, holding a plasma pistol in a futuristic holster, looking at a shimmering energy field, low-angle shot. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: Lena Petrova, in a dynamic pose, striking a masculine guard (a clearly adult man with a beard) in a chaotic control room, sparks flying from a console she is hotwiring, mid-action, close-up on her determined face. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: Lena Petrova, looking exhausted but victorious, standing amidst the deactivated remnants of the lab, a deactivated rocket in the background, a small smile on her face, wide shot from behind, looking out at the vastness. All panels feature cel-shading, bold ink outlines, and cinematic lighting. NO text, NO speech bubbles, NO dialogue, NO children.

definitions.definition: Doubt; the condition of being uncertain or without conviction.
Something uncertain or ambiguous.
A parameter that measures the dispersion of a range of measured values.
The action of mocking; ridicule, derision.
Something so lacking in necessary qualities as to inspire ridicule; a laughing-stock.
Something insultingly imitative; an offensively futile action, gesture etc.
Mimicry, imitation, now usually in a derogatory sense; a travesty, a ridiculous simulacrum.
definitions.example: The defendant wasn't allowed to speak at his own trial - it was a mockery of justice.

definitions.definition: A building or institution dedicated to the acquisition, conservation, study, exhibition, and educational interpretation of objects having scientific, historical, cultural or artistic value.

Any member of the clade Simiiformes not also of the clade Hominoidea containing humans and apes, from which they are usually, but not universally, distinguished by smaller size, a tail, and cheek pouches.
definitions.example: He had been visiting an area zoo when a monkey swung from its tree perch, swiped his glasses and hurled them into a hippo hole.

definitions.definition: Any nonhuman simian primate, including apes.
definitions.example: Chimpanzees are known to form bands to hunt and kill other monkeys.

definitions.definition: A mischievous child.
definitions.example: She's a cheeky monkey.

definitions.definition: A dance move popular in the 1960s.
Five hundred pounds sterling; five hundred dollars.
A person or the role of the person on the sidecar platform of a motorcycle involved in sidecar racing.
A person with minimal intelligence and/or an unattractive appearance
A face card.
A menial employee who does a repetitive job, as in code monkey, grease monkey, phone monkey, powder monkey.

The weight or hammer of a pile driver; a heavy mass of iron, which, being raised high, falls on the head of the pile, and drives it into the earth; the falling weight of a drop hammer used in forging.
A small trading vessel of the sixteenth century.
A drug habit; an addiction; a compulsion.
A fluid consisting of hydrochloric acid and zinc, used in the process of soldering.
A person's temper, said to be "up" when they are angry.
A black person.
To meddle; to mess (with).
definitions.example: Please don't monkey with the controls if you don't know what you're doing.

definitions.definition: To mimic; to ape.
A vessel or aircraft that serves or carries a smaller vessel or vessels that operate independently from it.
Fond term of address for one's mother.
The state of being a mother; motherhood
The state or characteristic of being just or fair.
definitions.example: the justice of a description

definitions.definition: The ideal of fairness, impartiality, etc., especially with regard to the punishment of wrongdoing.
definitions.example: Justice was served.

definitions.definition: Judgment and punishment of a party who has allegedly wronged another.
definitions.example: to demand justice

definitions.definition: The civil power dealing with law.
definitions.example: Ministry of Justice

definitions.definition: A title given to judges of certain courts; capitalized as a title.
definitions.example: Mr. Justice Krever presides over the appellate court

definitions.definition: Correctness, conforming to reality or rules.
To show (someone) the way; to guide, conduct; to point, indicate.
definitions.example: ‘The bliss is there’, mumbled the old man and taught to Heaven.

definitions.definition: (ditransitive) To pass on knowledge to.
definitions.example: Can you teach me to sew?  Can you teach sewing to me?

definitions.definition: To pass on knowledge, especially as one's profession; to act as a teacher.
definitions.example: She used to teach at university.

definitions.definition: (ditransitive) To cause to learn or understand.
(ditransitive) To cause to know the disagreeable consequences of some action.
definitions.example: I'll teach you to make fun of me!

definitions.definition: An electric ray of the genus Torpedo.
A cylindrical explosive projectile that can travel underwater and is used as a weapon.
A submarine sandwich.
A naval mine.
An explosive device buried underground and set off remotely, to destroy fortifications, troops, or cavalry; a land torpedo.
A professional gunman or assassin.
A small explosive device attached to the top of the rail to provide an audible warning when a train passes over it.
A kind of firework in the form of a small ball, or pellet, which explodes when thrown upon a hard object.
An automobile with a torpedo body.
(usually in the plural) A woman's shoe with a pointed toe.
(usually in the plural) A large breast; breast with a large nipple.
A marijuana cigarette.
To send a torpedo, usually from a submarine, that explodes below the waterline of the target ship.
To sink a ship with one of more torpedoes.
To undermine or destroy any endeavor with a stealthy, powerful attack.
An often childish display or fit of bad temper.
definitions.example: Baby Shawn threw a tantrum when he was told the bicycle was not his.

definitions.definition: To throw a tantrum.
To be adjacent to
definitions.example: Though France neighbours Germany, its culture is significantly different.

definitions.definition: (followed by "on"; figurative) To be similar to, to be almost the same as.
definitions.example: That sort of talk is neighbouring on treason.

definitions.definition: To associate intimately with; to be close to.
A small nymph.
A sexually attractive girl or young woman.
A device for taking still or moving pictures or photographs.
The viewpoint in a three-dimensional game or simulation.
A vaulted room.
The judge's private chamber, where cases may be heard in camera.
One of the substances present in a mixture, especially food.
(systems theory) An underlying base or foundation especially for an organization or system.
definitions.example: The parasitic tyranny's infrastructure depends on secrecy in order to be effective.

definitions.definition: The basic facilities, services and installations needed for the functioning of a community or society.
To give up or relinquish control of, to surrender or to give oneself over, or to yield to one's emotions.
To desist in doing, practicing, following, holding, or adhering to; to turn away from; to permit to lapse; to renounce; to discontinue.
To leave behind; to desert as in a ship or a position, typically in response to overwhelming odds or impending dangers; to forsake, in spite of a duty or responsibility.
definitions.example: Many baby girls have been abandoned on the streets of Beijing.

definitions.definition: To subdue; to take control of.
To cast out; to banish; to expel; to reject.
To no longer exercise a right, title, or interest, especially with no interest of reclaiming it again; to yield; to relinquish.
To surrender to the insurer (an insured item), so as to claim a total loss.
To succeed in something, now especially in academic performance.
To carry out successfully; to accomplish.
To conclude, finish, especially successfully.
To obtain, or gain (a desired result, objective etc.), as the result of exertion; to succeed in gaining; to win.
To conclude, to turn out.
To obtain (a material thing).
definitions.example: Show all the spoils by valiant kings achieved.

definitions.definition: (of a goal or status) Having been reached, attained or accomplished.
To suffer pain; to be the source of, or be in, pain, especially continued dull pain; to be distressed.
To cause someone or something to suffer pain.
The feeling of an ache; a dull pain.
That aches; continuously painful.
(usually groceries) retail foodstuffs and other household supplies.
A shop or store that sells groceries; a grocery store.
To go grocery shopping.
To furnish with groceries.
Having a broad range (of different elements).
definitions.example: The reasons are various.

definitions.definition: That varies or differs from others; variant; different.
definitions.example: a various reading of a Biblical text

definitions.definition: (heading) To interact socially.
(heading) To interact antagonistically.
(heading) To interact contractually.
(heading) To interact mechanically.
To enter into (an activity), to participate (construed with in).
To entangle.
Agreed to be married.
Busy or employed.
Greatly interested.
(of a telephone) Already involved in a telephone call when a third party calls
definitions.example: I tried calling, but she (or her phone) was engaged.

definitions.definition: (of a column) attached to a wall or sunk into it halfway
(of gears or cogs) in contact and in operation
Being attacked or attacking
(of a foetus) Having the widest part of its presenting part, usually the head, enter the pelvic brim or inlet.
One of the simplest or essential parts or principles of which anything consists, or upon which the constitution or fundamental powers of anything are based.
definitions.example: Letters are the elements of written language.

definitions.definition: A small part of the whole.
definitions.example: an element of doubt;  an element of the picture

definitions.definition: The sky.
(with "the") Atmospheric forces such as strong winds and rains.
definitions.example: exposed to the elements

definitions.definition: A place or state of being that an individual or object is best suited to.
definitions.example: to be in one's element

definitions.definition: (usually in the plural) The bread and wine taken at Holy Communion.
A group of people within a larger group having a particular common characteristic.
definitions.example: You sometimes find the hooligan element at football matches.

definitions.definition: A component in electrical equipment, often in the form of a coil, having a high resistance, thereby generating heat when a current is passed through it.
definitions.example: The element in this electric kettle can heat the water in under a minute.

definitions.definition: One of the conceptual objects in a markup language, usually represented in text by tags.
To compound of elements.
To constitute and be the elements of.
Of, or relating to sight; visual.
definitions.example: Strabismus is an optical defect.

definitions.definition: Designed to assist or enhance sight
definitions.example: A microscope is an optical instrument.

definitions.definition: Of, or relating to optics.
definitions.example: Refraction is an optical effect.

definitions.definition: Of, or relating to visible light.
definitions.example: Optical telescopes don't work when it is cloudy.

definitions.definition: Incorporating light-sensitive devices.
definitions.example: An optical switch opens the door automatically.

definitions.definition: A small increase or upward change in something that has been steady or declining.
A stock market transaction or quote at a price above a preceding one.
A very large video screen, typically used in a stadium or concert venue to show close-up shots.
To do or say something for amusement rather than seriously.
definitions.example: I didn’t mean what I said — I was only joking.

definitions.definition: (intransitive, followed by with) To dupe in a friendly manner for amusement; to mess with, play with.
definitions.example: Relax, man, I'm just joking with you.

definitions.definition: To make merry with; to make jokes upon; to rally.
definitions.example: to joke a comrade

definitions.definition: The act of telling or engaging in jokes.
A public official whose duty it is to administer the law, especially by presiding over trials and rendering judgments; a justice.
A person who decides the fate of someone or something that has been called into question.
A person officiating at a sports event or similar.
definitions.example: At a boxing match, the decision of the judges is final.

definitions.definition: A person who evaluates something or forms an opinion.
definitions.example: She is a good judge of wine.

definitions.definition: One of the sensations produced by the tongue in response to certain chemicals; the quality of giving this sensation.
definitions.example: He had a strange taste in his mouth.

definitions.definition: The sense that consists in the perception and interpretation of this sensation.
definitions.example: His taste was impaired by an illness.

definitions.definition: A small sample of food, drink, or recreational drugs.
A person's implicit set of preferences, especially esthetic, though also culinary, sartorial, etc.
definitions.example: Dr. Parker has good taste in wine.

definitions.definition: Personal preference; liking; predilection.
definitions.example: I have developed a taste for fine wine.

definitions.definition: A small amount of experience with something that gives a sense of its quality as a whole.
A kind of narrow and thin silk ribbon.
To sample the flavor of something orally.
To have a taste; to excite a particular sensation by which flavour is distinguished.
definitions.example: The chicken tasted great, but the milk tasted like garlic.

definitions.definition: To experience.
definitions.example: I tasted in her arms the delights of paradise.

definitions.definition: To take sparingly.
To try by eating a little; to eat a small quantity of.
To try by the touch; to handle.
A government in which a single ruler (a tyrant) has absolute power; this system of government.
The office or jurisdiction of an absolute ruler.
Absolute power, or its use.
A system of government in which power is exercised on behalf of the ruler or ruling class, without regard to the wishes of the governed.
Extreme severity or rigour.
To receive (something) from somebody temporarily, expecting to return it.
To take money from a bank under the agreement that the bank will be paid over the course of time.
To adopt (an idea) as one's own.
definitions.example: to borrow the style, manner, or opinions of another

definitions.definition: To adopt a word from another language.

In a subtraction, to deduct (one) from a digit of the minuend and add ten to the following digit, in order that the subtraction of a larger digit in the subtrahend from the digit in the minuend to which ten is added gives a positive result.
(Upper Midwestern United States) To lend.
To temporarily obtain (something) for (someone).
To feign or counterfeit.
(obsolete except in ballads) To secure the release of (someone) from prison.
To receive (something) from somebody, with little possibility of returning it.
definitions.example: Can I borrow a sheet of paper?

definitions.definition: A kind of needle, shorter than a sharp, with a small rounded eye, used for making fine stitches on heavy fabrics.
In the position or interval that separates (two things), or intermediate in quantity or degree. (See Usage notes below.)
definitions.example: I want to buy one that costs somewhere between forty and fifty dollars.

definitions.definition: Done together or reciprocally.
definitions.example: conversation between friends

definitions.definition: Shared in confidence.
definitions.example: Between you and me, I think the boss is crazy.  Let's keep this between ourselves.

definitions.definition: In transit from (one to the other, or connecting places).
definitions.example: He's between jobs right now.  The shuttle runs between the town and the airport.

definitions.definition: Combined (by effort or ownership).
definitions.example: Between the leaky taps and the peeling wallpaper, there isn't much about this house to appeal to a buyer.

definitions.definition: One of (representing a choice).
definitions.example: Some colour-blind people can't distinguish between red and green.

definitions.definition: A person's life story, especially one published.
definitions.example: There are many biographies of Benjamin Franklin.

definitions.definition: The art of writing this kind of story.
To write a biography of.
A nonaligned state, or a member of such a state.
A person who takes no side in a dispute.
An individual or entity serving as an arbitrator or adjudicator.
A neutral hue.
The position of a set of gears in which power cannot be transmitted to the drive mechanism.
An electrical terminal or conductor which has zero or close to zero voltage with respect to the ground.
Not taking sides in a conflict such as war; nonaligned.
Favouring neither the supporting nor opposing viewpoint of a topic of debate; unbiased.
definitions.example: I am neutral regarding the issue of gay marriage.

definitions.definition: (grammar) Neither positive nor negative.
Neither beneficial nor harmful.
Having no sex; neuter.
Having no obvious colour; gray
Neither positive nor negative; possessing no charge or equivalent positive and negative charge such that there is no imbalance.
Having a pH near 7, neither acidic nor alkaline.
The quality of being a neighbor, of living nearby, next to each-other; proximity.
definitions.example: Our neighborhood was our only reason to exchange hollow greetings.

definitions.definition: Close proximity, nearby area; particularly, close proximity to one's home.
definitions.example: He lives in my neighborhood.

definitions.definition: The inhabitants of a residential area.
definitions.example: The fire alarmed the neighborhood.

definitions.definition: A formal or informal division of a municipality or region.
definitions.example: We have just moved to a pleasant neighborhood.

definitions.definition: An approximate amount.
definitions.example: He must be making in the neighborhood of $200,000 per year.

definitions.definition: The quality of physical proximity.
definitions.example: The slums and the palace were in awful neighborhood.

definitions.definition: The disposition becoming a neighbor; neighborly kindness or good will.
Within a topological space:
Within a metric space:
The infinitesimal open set of all points that may be reached directly from a given point.
The set of all the vertices adjacent to a given vertex.
The act or process of calculating.
The result of calculating.
Reckoning, estimate.
definitions.example: By my calculation, we should be there by midnight.

definitions.definition: An expectation based on circumstances.
A non-genuine article; a fake.
One who counterfeits; a counterfeiter.
That which resembles another thing; a likeness; a portrait; a counterpart.
An impostor; a cheat.
To falsely produce what appears to be official or valid; to produce a forged copy of.
definitions.example: to counterfeit the signature of another, coins, notes, etc.

definitions.definition: To produce a faithful copy of.
To feign; to mimic.
definitions.example: to counterfeit the voice of another person

definitions.definition: (usually "be counterfeited") Of a turn or river card, to invalidate a player's hand by making a better hand on the board.
False, especially of money; intended to deceive or carry appearance of being genuine.
definitions.example: This counterfeit watch looks like the real thing, but it broke a week after I bought it.

definitions.definition: Inauthentic.
definitions.example: counterfeit sympathy

definitions.definition: Assuming the appearance of something; deceitful; hypocritical.
A belt or girdle.
To pierce or puncture slightly.
definitions.example: John hardly felt the needle prick his arm when the adept nurse drew blood.

definitions.definition: To form by piercing or puncturing.
definitions.example: to prick a pattern for embroidery

definitions.definition: To mark or denote by a puncture; to designate by pricking; to choose; to mark.
To mark the surface of (something) with pricks or dots; especially, to trace a ship’s course on (a chart).
To run a middle seam through the cloth of a sail.
To fix by the point; to attach or hang by puncturing.
definitions.example: to prick a knife into a board

definitions.definition: To be punctured; to suffer or feel a sharp pain, as by puncture.
definitions.example: A sore finger pricks.

definitions.definition: To make or become sharp; to erect into a point; to raise, as something pointed; said especially of the ears of an animal, such as a horse or dog; and usually followed by up.
definitions.example: The dog's ears pricked up at the sound of a whistle.

definitions.definition: Usually in the form prick out: to plant (seeds or seedlings) in holes made in soil at regular intervals.
To incite, stimulate, goad.
To urge one's horse on; to ride quickly.
To affect with sharp pain; to sting, as with remorse.
To make acidic or pungent.
To become sharp or acid; to turn sour, as wine.
To aim at a point or mark.
Usually as prick up: to dress or adorn; to prink.
Punctured by small holes
Something that is wrapped around something else as a cover or protection: a wrapping.
An outer garment; a loose robe or dressing gown.
One who, or that which, wraps.
definitions.example: He proved to be a remarkably efficient wrapper of parcels.

definitions.definition: A construct, such as a class or module, that serves to mediate access to another.
definitions.example: We need a Perl wrapper for this C++ library.

definitions.definition: A burst, split, or break.
A social breach or break, between individuals or groups.
A break or tear in soft tissue, such as a muscle.
A failure mode in which a tough ductile material pulls apart rather than cracking.
To burst, break through, or split, as under pressure.
To dehisce irregularly.
A veterinary surgeon, a veterinarian
Of or relating to the medical or surgical treatment of animals, especially domestic and farm animals.
(usually with definite article) Electricity; the electricity supply.
definitions.example: We had to sit in the dark because the electric was cut off.

definitions.definition: An electric car.
An electric toothbrush.
A substance or object which can be electrified; an insulator or non-conductor, like amber or glass.
Fencing with the use of a body wire, box, and related equipment to detect when a weapon has touched an opponent.
Of, relating to, produced by, operated with, or utilising electricity; electrical.
Of or relating to an electronic version of a musical instrument that has an acoustic equivalent.
Being emotionally thrilling; electrifying.
One who earns money.
A profitable product or scheme.
definitions.example: Those Japanese radios were a nice little earner: we sold all of them by lunchtime.

definitions.definition: Unambiguous; without equivocation or ambiguity; singularly clear, unmistakable, or unquestionable
definitions.example: I want you to give me an unequivocal guarantee on that.

definitions.definition: (possibly obsolete) Without equal, matchless.
definitions.example: the unequivocal scope of the Golden Gate Bridge

definitions.definition: The process of terminating or the state of being terminated.
The process of firing an employee; ending one's employment at a business for any reason.
An end in time; a conclusion.
An end in space; an edge or limit.
An outcome or result.
The last part of a word; an ending, a desinence; a suffix.
An induced abortion.
A word, a term.
The ending up of a polypeptid chain.
Of technology, a concept, etc, so far advanced as to appear to be from the future.
A case for carrying a tool, particularly a gun, safely and accessibly.
A belt with loops or slots for carrying small tools or other equipment.
To put something in a holster.
The act, quality, or condition of being honest.

definitions.definition: Honor; decency, propriety.
Chastity.
Any of various crucifers in the genus Lunaria, several of which are grown as ornamentals, particularly Lunaria annua.
One's social heritage, or previous life; what one did in the past.

definitions.definition: A part of the picture that depicts scenery to the rear or behind the main subject; context.
Information relevant to the current situation about past events; history.
A less important feature of scenery (as opposed to foreground).

definitions.definition: The image or color over which a computer's desktop items are shown (e.g. icons or application windows).
A type of activity on a computer that is not normally visible to the user.

definitions.definition: To put in a position that is not prominent.
To gather and provide background information (on).
(usually uncountable) Luggage; traveling equipment
definitions.example: Please put your baggage in the trunk.

definitions.definition: Factors, especially psychological ones, which interfere with a person's ability to function effectively.
definitions.example: This person has got a lot of emotional baggage.

definitions.definition: A woman.
An army's portable equipment; its baggage train.
(chiefly in the plural) A building for soldiers, especially within a garrison; originally referred to temporary huts, now usually to a permanent structure or set of buildings.
(chiefly in the plural) primitive structure resembling a long shed or barn for (usually temporary) housing or other purposes
(chiefly in the plural) any very plain, monotonous, or ugly large building
A movable roof sliding on four posts, to cover hay, straw, etc.
(usually in the plural) A police station.
To house military personnel; to quarter.
To live in barracks.
To jeer and heckle; to attempt to disconcert by verbal means.
To cheer for or support a team.
A group of buildings used by military personnel as housing.
Any plant of the genus Helianthus, so called probably from the form and color of its floral head, having the form of a large disk surrounded by yellow ray flowers.
A bright yellow, like that of the flower petals.
Any flat, radially symmetric organic compound such as coronene
The act of interrogating or questioning; examination by questions; inquiry.
A question put; an inquiry.
A question mark.
To yield, make or manufacture; to generate.

To sponsor and present (a motion picture, etc) to an audience or to the public.
To extend an area, or lengthen a line.

definitions.definition: To alter using technology, as opposed to simply performing.

definitions.definition: A guard dog

An individual or group that monitors the activities of another entity (such as an individual, corporation, non-profit group, or governmental organization) on behalf of the public to ensure that entity does not behave illegally or unethically.
To perform a function analogous to that of a watchdog; to guard and warn.
To be continuously reset by a watchdog timer.
To hit, slap or strike.
To kill, bump off.
To share or parcel out; often with up.

definitions.definition: To beat convincingly; to thrash.
(usually in the negative) To surpass; to better.
Tired; fatigued.

definitions.definition: Intoxicated on drugs.
Susceptibility to attack or injury; the state or condition of being weak or poorly defended.
definitions.example: The country recognized their defence vulnerability after an airplane landed in front of the central square without any consequences.

definitions.definition: A specific weakness in the protections or defences surrounding someone or something.
A weakness which allows an attacker to reduce a system's security
An outer part.
The 4th circle on a target, outside the inner and magpie.
A shot which strikes the outer of a target.
(retail) The smallest single unit sold by wholesalers to retailers, usually one retail display box.
Outside; external.
Farther from the centre of the inside.
Up to the time of (something happening).

definitions.definition: Up to (a certain place)
definitions.example: Keep walking until the second set of traffic lights, then turn left.

definitions.definition: Before (a time).
To; physically towards.
Up to the time that (a condition becomes true).
Before (a condition becoming true).
A distinctive outfit that serves to identify members of a group.
Phonetic equivalent for the letter U in the ICAO spelling alphabet, informally known as the NATO phonetic alphabet.
A uniformed police officer (as opposed to a detective).
To clothe in a uniform.
Unvarying; all the same.
Consistent; conforming to one standard.
With speed of convergence not depending on choice of function argument; as in uniform continuity, uniform convergence
(of a polymer) Composed of a single macromolecular species.
(of a polyhedron) That is isogonal and whose faces are regular polygons; (of an n-dimensional (n>3) polytope) that is isogonal and whose bounding (n-1)-dimensional facets are uniform polytopes.
A very short, unspecified length of time.

The length of an alternating current power cycle (1/60 or 1/50 of a second).
The time taken for light to travel a specified distance in a vacuum, usually one centimetre, but sometimes one foot or the width of a nucleon.
Short for jiffy bag, a padded envelope.
A cloth used for wiping, especially one used for drying anything wet, as a person after a bath.
To hit with a towel.
To dry by using a towel.

definitions.definition: To block up (a door, etc.) with a towel, to conceal the fumes of a recreational drug.
To beat with a stick.
Lacking in courage or confidence.

definitions.definition: A warder or jailer/gaoler; keeper of the keys in a prison.

definitions.example: They wanted a turnkey solution for the entire system, but we could only provide the enclosure.

definitions.definition: To hit or strike violently; to deliver a blow to.
To throw.
The science and technology of creating nanoparticles and of manufacturing machines which have sizes within the range of nanometres.
One who protects or supports; a defender or advocate.
An influential, wealthy person who supported an artist, craftsman, a scholar or a noble.

One who has gift and disposition of a benefice.
A padrone.
A property owner, a landlord, a master. (Compare patroon.)
To be a patron of; to patronize; to favour.
To treat as a patron.
Something that is owned.

definitions.definition: A piece of real estate, such as a parcel of land.

definitions.definition: Real estate; the business of selling houses.

definitions.definition: The exclusive right of possessing, enjoying and disposing of a thing.

definitions.definition: An attribute or abstract quality which is characteristic of a class of objects.

definitions.definition: An editable or read-only parameter associated with an application, component or class, or the value of such a parameter.

definitions.definition: (usually in the plural) A prop, an object used in a dramatic production.

definitions.definition: A measure of the effectiveness that a force producing a physical effect has over time. If linear, the quotient of: (force multiplied by the displacement of or in an object) ÷ time. If rotational, the quotient of: (force multiplied by the angle of displacement) ÷ time.

A product of equal factors (and generalizations of this notion): x^n, read as "x to the power of n" or the like, is called a power and denotes the product x \times x \times \cdots \times x, where x appears n times in the product; x is called the base and n the exponent.
Cardinality.
The probability that a statistical test will reject the null hypothesis when the alternative hypothesis is true.
(in plural) In Christian angelology, an intermediate level of angels, ranked above archangels, but exact position varies by classification scheme.

definitions.definition: To hit or kick something forcefully.

definitions.definition: A waste land; an uninhabited desolate region; a wilderness or desert.
A place that has been laid waste or destroyed.

A vast expanse of water.
A disused mine or part of one.
The action or progress of wasting; extravagant consumption or ineffectual use.

definitions.definition: Large abundance of something, specifically without it being used.
Gradual loss or decay.
A decaying of the body by disease; wasting away.

definitions.definition: A subdivision of an organization.

A territorial division; a district; especially, in France, one of the districts into which the country is divided for governmental purposes, similar to a county in the UK and in the USA. France is composed of 101 départements organized in 18 régions, each department is divided into arrondissements, in turn divided into cantons.

definitions.definition: Act of departing; departure.
(grammar) The masculine gender.
(grammar) A word of the masculine gender.
That which is masculine.

Of or pertaining to the male gender; manly.
Of or pertaining to the male sex; biologically male, not female.
Belonging to males; typically used by males.

definitions.definition: Having the qualities stereotypically associated with men: virile, aggressive, not effeminate.
(grammar) Of, pertaining or belonging to the male grammatical gender, in languages that have gender distinctions.
An elevation of land of considerable dimensions rising more or less abruptly, forming a conspicuous figure in the landscape, usually having a small extent of surface at its summit.

definitions.definition: Something very large in size or quantity; a huge amount; a great heap.

definitions.definition: Teaching children at home instead of sending them to school.
